YEAR.description: Four-digit year identifying the model version (e.g., 1970, 2008, 2020)

TIMCcsdsTimeCode.description: CCSDS time-code payload and preamble metadata.

PREAMBLE_FIELD2.description: Second CCSDS preamble field octet; ignored when not signaled by preamble 1.

AGENCY_DEFINED_EPOCH_ISO8601.description: Optional agency-defined epoch timestamp for agency epoch time codes.

CCSDS_EPOCH_ISO8601.description: Optional CCSDS epoch override timestamp; defaults to 1958-01-01T00:00:00 TAI.

TIMInstant.description: Numeric or textual instant tagged with its time system and representation.

EPOCH_FORMAT.description: Interpretation of JULIAN_DATE, SECONDS, and ISO8601.

JULIAN_DATE.description: Julian Date or Modified Julian Date day value, selected by EPOCH_FORMAT.

SECONDS.description: Seconds for UNIX_SECONDS, GPS_SECONDS, GNSS_WEEK_SECONDS, or MISSION_ELAPSED_SECONDS.

ISO8601.description: ISO 8601 timestamp text for ISO8601 inputs or display outputs.

SUBSECOND_NANOS.description: Additional nanoseconds beyond the fractional scalar/text value.

EPOCH_LABEL.description: Optional application-defined epoch label for MET, MRT, or SCLK values.

GNSS_WEEK.description: GNSS week number when EPOCH_FORMAT is GNSS_WEEK_SECONDS.

GNSS_ROLLOVER_REFERENCE_ISO8601.description: Optional Orekit-style GNSS week rollover reference timestamp.

CCSDS_TIME_CODE.description: CCSDS time-code payload when EPOCH_FORMAT is CCSDS_TIME_CODE.

TIMConversionRequest.description: Request to convert one instant into another time system/representation.

TAI_MINUS_UTC_SECONDS.description: Optional TAI-UTC override in seconds for the source instant.

HAS_TAI_MINUS_UTC.description: Whether TAI_MINUS_UTC_SECONDS should override runtime leap-second data.

DUT1_SECONDS.description: Optional UT1-UTC override in seconds for UT1 conversions.

HAS_DUT1.description: Whether DUT1_SECONDS should override runtime Earth-orientation data.

TIMConversionResult.description: Result of a time conversion request.

DELTA_SECONDS.description: Target minus source offset in SI seconds for the requested conversion.

ERROR_MESSAGE.description: Optional error detail when STATUS is not OK.

TRACE_ID.description: Caller trace/correlation identifier copied from the request when present.

TIME_SYSTEM.description: Legacy time-system selector retained for existing TIM consumers.

FrequencyRange.description: Frequency range with lower and upper limits

StokesParameters.description: Stokes parameters, representing different aspects of polarization

U.description: Another linear polarization, orthogonal to Q

Band.description: Table representing a frequency band with a name and frequency range

DATA_MODE.description: Mode of the data (real, simulated, synthetic)

POWER_TYPE.description: Type of power (eg. AC or DC)

TRANSMIT.description: Indicates if the EMT can transmit

RECEIVE.description: Indicates if the EMT can receive

TASKABLE.description: Indicates if the site is taskable

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Object type (Payload, Rocket body, Debris, Unknown)

RCS.description: Radar Cross Section [meters2]; blank if no data available

ORBIT_TYPE.description: Orbit type (Orbit, Landing, Impact, Docked to RSO, roundtrip)

MANEUVERABLE.description: Indicates if the object is maneuverable

SIZE.description: Size [meters]; blank if no data available

MASS.description: Mass [kilograms]; blank if no data available

PPEPositionRecord.description:
A single time-segment record of polynomial coefficients for Cartesian position
(and optionally velocity). Coefficients are stored per axis.

To evaluate position at time t within this record's validity window:
1. Compute normalized time: tau = (t - EPOCH_MID) / EPOCH_HALF_SPAN
where tau is in [-1, +1].
2. Evaluate the polynomial basis of degree (NUM_COEFFICIENTS - 1) using
the coefficients for each axis.

Velocity coefficients, if present, follow the same evaluation procedure.
If HAS_VELOCITY_COEFFICIENTS is false, velocity can be obtained by
analytically differentiating the position polynomial.

EPOCH_MID.description:
Midpoint epoch of this record's validity window (ISO 8601 UTC or TDB).
Together with EPOCH_HALF_SPAN, defines the time interval:
[EPOCH_MID - EPOCH_HALF_SPAN, EPOCH_MID + EPOCH_HALF_SPAN]

EPOCH_HALF_SPAN.description:
Half-span of the validity window in seconds.
The full span is 2 * EPOCH_HALF_SPAN seconds centered on EPOCH_MID.

NUM_COEFFICIENTS.description:
Number of polynomial coefficients per axis.
The polynomial degree is (NUM_COEFFICIENTS - 1).
Typical values: 8-32 for high-fidelity ephemeris fits.

BASIS_TYPE.description: Polynomial basis type for interpreting the coefficient arrays.

POS_COEFF_X.description:
Position coefficients for X-axis (km).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS. Ordered c0, c1, ..., c_{N-1}.

POS_COEFF_Y.description:
Position coefficients for Y-axis (km).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

POS_COEFF_Z.description:
Position coefficients for Z-axis (km).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

HAS_VELOCITY_COEFFICIENTS.description:
Whether explicit velocity coefficients are provided.
If false, velocity should be derived by differentiating the position polynomial.

VEL_COEFF_X.description:
Velocity coefficients for X-axis (km/s). Optional.
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS if present.

MAX_POSITION_RESIDUAL.description: Maximum position fit residual over this segment (km). Optional quality metric.

RMS_POSITION_RESIDUAL.description: RMS position fit residual over this segment (km). Optional quality metric.

PPEOrbitalElementRecord.description:
A single time-segment record of polynomial coefficients for classical orbital elements.

The six classical elements are:
1. SMA or R_PERIAPSIS (size/shape) — see SIZE_SHAPE_TYPE
2. Eccentricity (dimensionless)
3. Inclination (degrees)
4. Right Ascension of Ascending Node / RAAN (degrees)
5. Argument of Periapsis (degrees)
6. Anomaly (degrees) — see ANOMALY_TYPE

Evaluation follows the same normalized-time procedure as PPEPositionRecord.

EPOCH_MID.description: Midpoint epoch of this record's validity window (ISO 8601 UTC or TDB).

EPOCH_HALF_SPAN.description: Half-span of the validity window in seconds.

NUM_COEFFICIENTS.description: Number of polynomial coefficients per element.

BASIS_TYPE.description: Polynomial basis type for interpreting the coefficient arrays.

SIZE_SHAPE_TYPE.description: Parameterization of the first orbital element (SMA vs R_PERIAPSIS).

ANOMALY_TYPE.description: Anomaly type for the sixth orbital element.

COEFF_SIZE_SHAPE.description:
Coefficients for SMA or radius of periapsis (km).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_ECCENTRICITY.description:
Coefficients for eccentricity (dimensionless).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_INCLINATION.description:
Coefficients for inclination (degrees).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_RAAN.description:
Coefficients for RAAN (degrees).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_ARG_PERIAPSIS.description:
Coefficients for argument of periapsis (degrees).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_ANOMALY.description:
Coefficients for anomaly (degrees). See ANOMALY_TYPE for interpretation.
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

MAX_ELEMENT_RESIDUAL.description:
Maximum element fit residual over this segment. Optional quality metric.
Units depend on the element (km for SMA, degrees for angles, dimensionless for ecc).

RMS_ELEMENT_RESIDUAL.description: RMS element fit residual over this segment. Optional quality metric.

PPE.description:
Polynomial Ephemeris — top-level message containing metadata and
one or more polynomial coefficient records for a single space object.

A PPE message may contain position records, orbital element records, or both.
Records should be ordered chronologically by EPOCH_MID and should collectively
cover the time span [START_TIME, STOP_TIME] without gaps.

CENTER_NAME.description: Origin of the reference frame (e.g., EARTH, MOON, MARS).

TIME_SYSTEM.description: Time system used for all epochs in this message.

START_TIME.description: Start of the total time span covered by this ephemeris (ISO 8601).

STOP_TIME.description: End of the total time span covered by this ephemeris (ISO 8601).

DEFAULT_BASIS_TYPE.description:
Default polynomial basis type for all records in this message.
Individual records may override this with their own BASIS_TYPE field.

POSITION_RECORDS.description:
Array of position polynomial records.
Each record covers a time segment; together they span [START_TIME, STOP_TIME].

ORBITAL_ELEMENT_RECORDS.description:
Array of orbital element polynomial records.
Each record covers a time segment; together they span [START_TIME, STOP_TIME].

EPHEMERIS_SOURCE.description: Generating ephemeris source (e.g., "JPL DE440", "HPOP v2.1", "Basilisk chebyPosEphem").

NOMINAL_SEGMENT_SPAN.description:
Fit span in seconds used to generate each polynomial segment.
Informational; actual spans are in individual records.

NOMINAL_NUM_COEFFICIENTS.description:
Nominal number of coefficients per segment.
Informational; actual counts are in individual records.

CCSDS_OCM_VERS.description: Format version in the form of 'x.y', where 'y' is incremented for minor changes, and 'x' for major changes.

COMMENT.description: Comments (a contiguous set of one or more comment lines may be provided immediately after the version number).

CLASSIFICATION.description: User-defined free-text message classification/caveats of this OCM.

MESSAGE_ID.description: Free-text field containing an ID that uniquely identifies a message from this originator.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Free-text field containing the name of the object.

INTERNATIONAL_DESIGNATOR.description: International designator for the object as assigned by COSPAR.

CATALOG_NAME.description: Satellite catalog source from which 'OBJECT_DESIGNATOR' was obtained.

OBJECT_DESIGNATOR.description: Unique satellite identification designator from the catalog.

ALTERNATE_NAMES.description: Alternate names of this space object.

ORIGINATOR_POC.description: Originator or programmatic Point-of-Contact (PoC) for OCM.

ORIGINATOR_POSITION.description: Contact position of the originator PoC.

TECH_ORG.description: Creating agency or operator for technical contact.

TECH_POSITION.description: Contact position of the technical PoC.

PREVIOUS_MESSAGE_ID.description: ID that uniquely identifies the previous message from this originator.

NEXT_MESSAGE_ID.description: ID that uniquely identifies the next message from this originator.

ADM_MSG_LINK.description: Unique identifier of Attitude Data Messages linked to this OCM.

CDM_MSG_LINK.description: Unique identifier of Conjunction Data Messages linked to this OCM.

PRM_MSG_LINK.description: Unique identifier of Pointing Request Messages linked to this OCM.

RDM_MSG_LINK.description: Unique identifier of Reentry Data Messages linked to this OCM.

TDM_MSG_LINK.description: List of Tracking Data Messages upon which this orbit determination is based.

COUNTRY.description: Country where the space object owner is based.

CONSTELLATION.description: Name of the constellation to which this space object belongs.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Specification of the type of object (e.g., PAYLOAD, ROCKET BODY, DEBRIS).

TIME_SYSTEM.description: Time system for all absolute time stamps in this OCM, including EPOCH_TZERO.

EPOCH_TZERO.description: Default epoch to which all relative times are referenced in data blocks.

OPS_STATUS.description: Specification of the operational status of the space object.

ORBIT_CATEGORY.description: Specification of the type of orbit (e.g., GEO, LEO).

OCM_DATA_ELEMENTS.description: List of elements of information data blocks included in this message.

SCLK_OFFSET_AT_EPOCH.description: Number of spacecraft clock counts at EPOCH_TZERO (used if TIME_SYSTEM is SCLK).

SCLK_SEC_PER_SI_SEC.description: Number of clock seconds occurring during one SI second (used if TIME_SYSTEM is SCLK).

PREVIOUS_MESSAGE_EPOCH.description: Creation epoch of the previous message from this originator.

NEXT_MESSAGE_EPOCH.description: Anticipated (or actual) epoch of the next message from this originator.

START_TIME.description: Time of the earliest data contained in the OCM.

STOP_TIME.description: Time of the latest data contained in the OCM.

TIME_SPAN.description: Span of time that the OCM covers, measured in days.

TAIMUTC_AT_TZERO.description: Difference (TAI - UTC) in seconds at EPOCH_TZERO.

NEXT_LEAP_TAIMUTC.description: Difference (TAI - UTC) in seconds at NEXT_LEAP_EPOCH.

UT1MUTC_AT_TZERO.description: Difference (UT1 - UTC) in seconds at EPOCH_TZERO.

EOP_SOURCE.description: Source and version of Earth Orientation Parameters used.

INTERP_METHOD_EOP.description: Method used to select or interpolate sequential EOP data.

CELESTIAL_SOURCE.description: Source and version of celestial body ephemeris data used.

X.description: X component of position in the specified reference frame.

COMMENT.description: Comments in the Physical Properties section.

WET_MASS.description: Wet mass of the space object.

DRY_MASS.description: Dry mass of the space object.

OEB_Q1.description: Quaternion component q1 of orientation from OEB to EME2000.

OEB_MAX.description: Maximum dimension of the object along OEB frame axes.

OEB_INT.description: Intermediate dimension along OEB frame axes.

OEB_MIN.description: Minimum dimension of the object along OEB frame axes.

SRP_CONST_AREA.description: Constant area for solar radiation pressure computations.

SRP_UNCERTAINTY.description: Uncertainty in the solar radiation pressure coefficient.

GM.description: Gravitational constant times the mass of the central body.

N_BODY_PERTURBATIONS.description: List of celestial bodies included in n-body perturbations.

FIXED_F10P7.description: Fixed F10.7 solar flux value used.

FIXED_F10P7_MEAN.description: Fixed mean F10.7 solar flux value used.

MAN_BASIS.description: Basis of the maneuver plan (e.g., planned, predicted, estimated).

MAN_REF_FRAME.description: Reference frame for the maneuver data.

MAN_FRAME_EPOCH.description: Epoch of the maneuver reference frame.

MAN_UNITS.description: Units for the maneuver data values.

OD_ID.description: Unique identifier for the orbit determination.

OD_PREV_ID.description: Identifier of the previous orbit determination.

OD_TIME_TAG.description: Time tag of the orbit determination.

OD_OBSERVATIONS_TYPE.description: Types of observations used (e.g., RANGE, DOPPLER).

OD_APRIORI_DATA.description: A priori data used for orbit determination.

OD_ESTIMATOR.description: Estimator class provenance: batch fit vs sequential filter (EKF/UKF).

OD_RESIDUAL_RMS.description: RMS of post-fit residuals for this solution.

OD_RESIDUALS_SERIES.description: Post-fit residual series, one entry per accepted observation.

OD_RESIDUAL_EPOCHS.description:
Epochs aligned with OD_RESIDUALS_SERIES (UNIX timestamp) [numeric seconds
since 1970-01-01T00:00:00 UTC].

OD_BATCH_BASELINE_ID.description:
OD_ID of the batch baseline solution a filtered solution derives from and
is compared against (empty for batch solutions).

OD_BATCH_BASELINE_RMS.description:
Post-fit residual RMS of the batch baseline, for direct batch-vs-filter
comparison.

TRAJ_TYPE.description:
Trajectory state representation type.
Determines how orbit state data is parameterized in this message.
For CARTESIAN_PV/CARTESIAN_PVA, use STATE_DATA array.
For POLYNOMIAL_POS/POLYNOMIAL_OE, use the corresponding polynomial record arrays.

TRAJ_TYPE_DESCRIPTION.description:
Legacy trajectory type string for backward compatibility and extended types
(e.g., "PROPAGATED", "ESTIMATED", "FILTERED").

STATE_STEP_SIZE.description: Time interval between state vectors in seconds (required for time-series data).

STATE_VECTOR_SIZE.description:
Number of components per state vector.
6 = position + velocity (X, Y, Z, X_DOT, Y_DOT, Z_DOT)
9 = position + velocity + acceleration (adds X_DDOT, Y_DDOT, Z_DDOT)

STATE_DATA.description:
State data as row-major array of doubles.
Layout: [X0, Y0, Z0, X_DOT0, Y_DOT0, Z_DOT0, X1, Y1, Z1, ...]
Time reconstruction: epoch[i] = METADATA.START_TIME + (i * STATE_STEP_SIZE)
Length must be divisible by STATE_VECTOR_SIZE.

COVARIANCE_DATA.description:
Covariance data as flat array (21 elements per epoch for 6x6 lower triangular).
Time alignment matches STATE_DATA epochs.

POLYNOMIAL_POSITION_RECORDS.description:
Polynomial position records.
Used when TRAJ_TYPE is POLYNOMIAL_POS. Each record covers a time segment
with polynomial coefficients for X, Y, Z position (and optionally velocity).
See PPE schema for record structure and evaluation procedure.

POLYNOMIAL_OE_RECORDS.description:
Polynomial orbital element records.
Used when TRAJ_TYPE is POLYNOMIAL_OE. Each record covers a time segment
with polynomial coefficients for classical orbital elements.
See PPE schema for record structure and evaluation procedure.

PHYSICAL_PROPERTIES.description: Physical properties of the space object.
